creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 1: Software Factories & Keynotes
videos.summary: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 1 livestream from Moscone West in San Francisco on June 30, 2026. Programming includes keynote sessions and main-stage talks focused on software factories and AI engineering topics.

videos.title: Building Great Agent Skills: The Missing Manual
videos.summary: The Skill Checklist Framework provides a structured approach to building high-quality agent skills by addressing four dimensions: Trigger (user-invoked vs model-invoked), Structure (steps and references), Steering (leading words to guide agent reasoning), and Pruning (removing obsolete or ineffective instructions). The framework aims to help developers escape "skill hell" by establishing shared rubrics for effective and maintainable skill development.

videos.title: Frontier results, on device - RL Nabors, Arize
videos.summary: RL Nabors argues that most AI applications default to expensive frontier models when small local models would suffice, incurring unnecessary latency and cost. He presents a framework for evaluating model performance using capability evals, golden datasets, and LLM-as-judge to decide when to use large remote models versus small local ones, demonstrated through production examples in agentic systems, moderation classifiers, and summarization.

videos.title: The Future Is Domain-Specific Agents - Justin Schroeder, StandardAgents
videos.summary: Justin Schroeder argues that domain-specific agents composed together represent the future of practical AI, claiming this architecture enables smaller models to perform real work and transition AI from internal tools to customer-facing products. He suggests composition over inheritance is the key architectural principle unlocking useful AI, with each domain agent more powerful than a skill and more reliable and cost-effective when combined.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Anthropic Just Released Claude Sonnet 5 and it's VERY IMPRESSIVE!
videos.summary: Anthropic released Claude Sonnet 5, which the creator characterizes as an excellent model for its cost. The video discusses the model's capabilities and presents a positive assessment of its performance relative to pricing.

videos.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
videos.summary: Cole Medin discusses Google's 50-page playbook on agentic engineering, arguing that while model selection is only 10% of results, the remaining 90% comes from system architecture (context, tools, guardrails, verification). The video breaks down the shift from "vibe coding" to structured agentic engineering and explains how skills in context engineering and orchestration will determine AI coding success in 2026.

videos.title: My AI Memory Now Follows Me Across Every Tool!
videos.summary: Walrus Memory is a memory layer that persists across different AI agents and tools, storing encrypted memories owned by the user. It enables context continuity when switching between different LLM providers like Claude, Codex, and Pi through a single-prompt setup.

videos.title: The New Kimi K2.7 Model is VERY Impressive
videos.summary: Kimi K2.7 Code is an open-source, coding-focused agentic model from Moonshot AI. The reviewer highlights its impressive performance relative to its size and cost.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: I was giving my coding agent context the wrong way...
videos.summary: AI Jason describes a lesson learned about providing context to coding agents, suggesting his previous approach was ineffective. He shares a codebase harness tool and references resources for learning agent implementation techniques.

videos.title: OpenClaw Creator's new secret project...
videos.summary: AI Jason presents a new project by OpenClaw Creator involving Crabbox and Codebase harness setup. A GitHub repository with skills configuration is provided, along with references to AI Builder Club training and Superdesign platform.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: AI Jason presents Loop Engineer, a framework or tool for building AI agents. The video covers what Loop Engineer is and provides setup instructions, with accompanying GitHub repository and workshop materials.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Loopany is an open-source framework for building proactive AI agents that can self-improve through loops. The video demonstrates how to use Loopany with memory and tools to create autonomous systems for applications like ad optimization and SEO.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: GPT 5.6 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: A video discusses OpenAI's announced GPT 5.6 model alongside Anthropic's Mythos 5 and Fable 5 models, noting that both OpenAI and Anthropic face regulatory constraints as the AI industry matures. OpenAI reportedly consolidated its model lineup to three offerings: Sol, Terra, and Luna.

videos.title: It's about time we learn Transformers..
videos.summary: Video tutorial explaining how generative pretrained transformers work, covering core components including self-attention, multi-head attention, positional embeddings, feed-forward networks, layer normalization, and residual connections. The content targets understanding GPT architecture as used in major models like Gemini, Opus, Qwen, and Kimi.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code covers Andrej Karpathy's explanation of the Bigram Language Model, a simple but instructive precursor to GPT. The video walks through tokenization, embeddings, loss functions, and optimization as core components of how language models process tokens.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series with Nano, Super, and Ultra variants featuring hybrid architecture combining Mamba, transformer layers, and mixture-of-experts techniques. The models employ Latent MoE and MTP components.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: Containers on Amazon ECS with Mama J
videos.summary: AWS Developers video explaining containers, Docker, and Amazon ECS as a container orchestration platform. Covers the "it works on my machine" problem, Docker image building, ECS orchestration, Fargate vs EC2 deployment options, and includes a shopping app example with auto-scaling for Black Friday scenarios.

videos.title: System Design for Beginners (Full Guide)
videos.summary: A system design interview guide emphasizing a 7-step framework that prioritizes problem understanding and requirements over technology selection. The framework sequences requirements gathering, traffic estimation, high-level components, API contracts, tech stack selection, and implementation constraints.

videos.title: Your AI Agent Needs a Tool
videos.summary: This tutorial explains why LLMs require external tools to move beyond generation-only behavior and demonstrates tool calling as the mechanism enabling agents to request and execute real-time operations. It covers tool schema, handler functions, and system prompt design for tool invocation.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS gives AI coding agents tools, knowledge, and guardrails to work with AWS services effectively. It provides up-to-date documentation, best practices, and tested procedures that improve success rates and reduce wasted effort compared to relying on agents' general knowledge.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Why is AI expensive all of a sudden?
videos.summary: Video explores why AI has become expensive, covering token pricing economics and the trend of "tokenmaxxing" where developers optimize code to consume more tokens. Discusses the real underlying reasons for rising AI costs with reference to industry analysis.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech explores whether Google engineers use code generation ("vibing code") and compares vibing practices across OpenAI Codex, Claude Code, and Google's own tools. The video examines code generation adoption at Google from 2024 to 2026.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech examines Malus, a company or tool positioned as disruptive to open source, discussing its implications for open source maintainers and the ecosystem. The video frames the topic as a provocative debate about whether Malus represents an existential threat to open source.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: He Built $100B at Google, Then Failed. Now He Runs an $80B+ Company | Snowflake, Sridhar Ramaswamy
videos.summary: Sridhar Ramaswamy, CEO of Snowflake, discusses his career arc from building Google's ad business to founding Neeva (a failed search engine startup) and now leading Snowflake's transition into the AI era. He shares lessons on leadership principles, hiring adaptable talent, staying close to product, and resilience after failure.

videos.title: Everything You Should Know Before Mythos Arrives | Nebulock, Damien Lewke
videos.summary: Damien Lewke, founder of Nebulock, argues that AI has drastically lowered attack costs, requiring defenders to adopt an "assume breach" posture and focus on detecting behavioral signals of compromise. He contends that most stages of the cyber kill chain are now automated and accessible, making proactive threat hunting critical compared to reactive alerting.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov, CEO of Gong, argues that software moats rely more on customer context, proprietary data, and operational scale than code alone. He contends that the best AI products create customer loyalty and that fears of AI disrupting SaaS companies are overstated.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that a forthcoming "ChatGPT Moment" in robotics will be driven by design for delight and human interaction rather than utility or humanoid form factors alone. She critiques the current focus on humanoid robots from companies like 1X and Unitree as a myopic vision that misses the broader future of billions of robots designed for meaningful human engagement.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: AI vs. Dedicated Software: The Real Efficiency Debate #shorts
videos.summary: A YouTube short by Cyril Imhof examines whether integrating AI for every task delivers long-term efficiency gains or represents a costly trap. The video questions whether dedicated software solutions still outperform general AI approaches despite current enthusiasm.

videos.title: Germany Heatwave MELTED my Ice Cream in Lisbon! #shorts
videos.summary: A YouTube short documents extreme heat affecting Germany and Lisbon, with ice cream melting as a visible symptom. The video attributes the intensity to urban heat island effects in concrete-heavy cities and frames it within broader climate change discourse.

videos.title: AI Learns, Takes Initiative, Works Asynchronously: The Future is Here! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof presents a perspective on AI systems that learn, take initiative, and operate asynchronously in ambient mode. The piece frames these capabilities as demonstrating organizational learning and represents a near-future vision of AI autonomy.

videos.title: Claude Tag: AI's European Takeover in Microsoft Teams! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof explores Claude Tag's integration into Microsoft Teams and Slack, questioning whether the move represents a strategic marketing push for enterprise adoption. The commentary focuses on enterprise AI integration and SaaS strategy.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
